reviews.review_text:
Sniper Elite 5 was my second game in this stealth focused series, and it builds well on what made the previous entries great. Set in France during 1944 (WW2), the game offers a solid story, but the real focus is on its expanded gameplay mechanics.

If you’re new to the series, I’d recommend starting with Sniper Elite 3 or 4 first. Elite 5 introduces more advanced systems like traps and deeper stealth mechanics, which can feel overwhelming for beginners. I played mainly the single player campaign, and it delivers a strong experience with 9 large missions, each taking over an hour if you play strategically by exploring, looting, and completing objectives carefully.

The gameplay encourages patience, planning, and multiple approaches, making every mission feel rewarding.

Pricing: Often gets a 90% discount during sales, just like Elite 4, offering great value for the amount of content plus replayability.

Overall Score: 8.5/10

Never buy this on full price!!!! (PLEASE READ BEFORE BUY)

My problems: 
(JUMP TO THE 4th POINT FOR THE MOST IMPORTANT THING IF YOU WANT TO READ IT QUICK)

1.
The maps are boring ,rushed and dont feel like a sniper game, they basically force you to use your secondary weapons with silencer to complete harder missions and that is why many people comper it to Hitman WOA, but for example in Hitman you can play a map 10+ times and still have fun and that's a huge difference. This game is just inferior to his predesecors and lacking creativity. I could go deeper on how unfinished this game is but these are my main problems.

2.
I rarely see anybody talk about the state of the francise ,all of you who are talking about how well made this game is please play with the older sniper elite games and then you realise this game got nowhere in 10 years, Rebellion realises it in every couple of years in poorer condition to grab money, later on that. Basically the game got more expensive and the quality declined over the years. The removed the progression system, many gameplay elements and much more. No wounder why SE4 cost more. Typic AAA mentality, they trown out an unfinished game whit tons of DLCs. AND WHAT THEY DO?? fix the game?? NO, THEY REALISE ANOTHER GAME IN THE SAME CONDITION( i am talking about SE Resistence) which is just a clone of this "masterpiece". The muliplayer died out in the first 2 years and they didn't even tried to make it serve a purpose, they just copy-pasted it from SE4. And i really dont understand why good game mechanics , improvements that were introduced years ago ARE'T IN THE GAME, BECUSE THEY COULD JUST COPY-PASTED IT, IF THEY CARED....... 

3.
There is no proper story just a remake of the previos games with french environment, but i think the worst part is that Karl's character got really forgetabble.A well made kill cam can't carry a game for decades. But my apologies it has a story "THE NAZIS ARE DEVELOPING A DANGEROUS WEAPON" which is very unique. Sadly the game lost its personality and became the poor mans Hitman. One of the most reconisable marks of series is the Hitler mission which had potencial , but they fumbled it on optimaisation. They could asked a historian or someone who read a history book in his life for some historical detailes and accuracy. Typic Hoolywood ww2 product and i dont expect Rebellion to write RDR2 or Ucharthed like stories but they could done other things right or atleast tried. Sadly the game is on tracks to be on the same boat whit the  Far Cry series.

4.
The DLCs are ridiculously overpriced, for example a single map is 10€ or 1 silenced pistol is 4€ and the fact that you are already paying 50 for the base game is a joke. Rebellion is one of the greediest companies in the market. You pay 100€ and get cliché skins and maps and even then the game has 20 hour playtime. There is no new game modes and co-op great , but again ist the same as before and there is so few unlockable skins and weapons. The game looks great in the XBOX game pass or in the Steam store but that's everything it's good for.

In conclusion, if you want it buy the deluxe bundle when its on sale but i'd recomend SE3 or 4(not on full price) or Hitman and  if you are hardcore sniper SE V2 is also a great pick, but i think COD or BF also done a better job in this field.

I haven't played a Sniper Elite game since the Xbox 360 era, so coming back to the series after such a long time made Sniper Elite 5 a great return point.

As with every Sniper Elite game, the combat is incredibly addictive. The slow-motion kill cams never get old and always give that satisfying dopamine hit that makes you want “just one more shot.” I also really enjoyed the variety of weapons available, from pistols and SMGs to grenades and rifles.

One feature that stood out to me was the weapon customization. Being able to change both the appearance and stats of your weapons to match your playstyle adds a lot to the overall enjoyment of the game.

The multiplayer was easily my favourite part, especially the “Invasion” mode. The concept of invading another player’s campaign or being hunted yourself, is something I rarely see in other games. It adds a constant sense of tension knowing another real player could be tracking you down at any moment. I actually ended up spending more time in Invasion mode than in the campaign itself.

Overall, the game has a lot of strong features and delivers a consistently exciting experience. I would definitely recommend it to anyone who enjoys tactical shooters or stealth games.

Exactly what you would expect of Sniper Elite game, cool slow-mo shots of enemies being penetrated by sniper bullies, big open levels and basically really fun gameplay. This time it feels like there is much more emphasis on stealth since levels are riddled with dozens of enemies that will swarm you once the alarm is raised.

an old school campaign set in world war 2 and no in game betrayal, which has been over-used in games (in each of your jedi games for instance - both fallen order and the sequel).
i am not into pvp but the invasion mechanic is interesting and should be fun though i feel the maps in resistance look better for that. fortunately you can turn off invasions or allow only friends - and there is campaign co-op play.
and because i enjoy this game so much im getting the dlc - but others recommend you do so when on offer, so check others comments on reasons why, or look at the contents on steam and decide if its worth the price. my attitude is a little different to most. i feel if a game was worth my time playing it, and has dlc, its worth getting the dlc when i have the money.  this game is worth my time and money. 
i dont wait for a sale when i judge a game was worth the time i played it, but if it is on sale when i decide the dlc is worth getting then i will take that luck. i judge the dlc for this will add more playability to the game for me, in many ways, so its worth it for me. you will make your own value judgements of course. but you cant invade another player's campaign mission if you dont own that map!

Overall, a very good game.  Lots of great replay.  I just finished the campaign, but I'm not an online player, but just the campaign was well worth the cost.  Great detail--sometimes I just wanted to look around: wild flowers, beautiful light, etc.  The last full mission inside a destroyed town had incredible detail in destroyed buildings with rooms that had character, a church with plenty of rooms and places to hide.  Small negatives: sometimes too easy, long shots should require much more experience, in realistic mode you can shoot a bad guy repeatedly in the face, and he won't drop, you can shoot a guy right next to a group and the others will just wait around for their bullet to the head.  But, positives far outweigh the negatives.  Great game.
very worth it game need games like this so enjoying bullet cam realistic graphics and ai this feels like real thanks a lot im so satisfied

A really good Sniper Elite game that I put a lot of hours into on my Xbox in Axis Invasion. It has a great variety of game modes with lots of detailed cool features. The maps are all great and have a lot potential for different play styles. It never feels like it restricts you to one thing, but instead you can play how you want no matter the game mode or map.

One thing I hate on this game is the satchel charge esp in Chapter 7 where you have to blast the launch site door with satchel charge. They put a lot of doors and tanks to blow up with a satchel and you cannot complete the damn last objective because you have the search for a satchel charges in a big map. Poor design

TLDR: Game is good, only thing i don't like is the bastardized M1 Grarad

The game gets Chalanging early into the game, like Mission 2 / 3 early.

Campaign Raids can be disabled for those who struggle with strategy, timing and counter intelligence.
personality i have not had much trouble with campaign raiders, there has been a select few who where better than me, and one who caught me in a bad spot.
but if you play your cards right you can out play an enemy and hit the Nazi Empire with a Black Eye.

Game rewards stealth by REQUIRING you to complete challanges to unlock new weapons.
Challanges being killing a designated target on each map in a designated way.

Weapon Customisation makes a return too and much similar to weapon unlocks,
Weapon Attachments Reward Exploration of maps to find them.

Sniper Elite 5's maps are big and open but now they are TALL too so make sure to keep your eyes open for enemy snipers.
along with the added height with every map, there are more close quarters sections.

Game Preformence is great for how big the maps are. you will only notice your PC's short Resources if your watching a Youtube video in the Backround on a monitor. (constantly hitting lows of 20 FPS and Highs of 60. I Recommend setting a Backround FPS Limit of the minimum 20 FPS in the NVIDIA Control panel if you insist on watching and playing.) 

so far i dont think im missing anything. this is all i have noticed so far while playing SE5, i will update this review if anything changes but i love it so far.

loved the game overall as i'm a huge fan of the serie, great experience as well but minor inconvience in the campaign with a bug that spoiled my experience a little since I didn't had explosives charges but had documents that were supposed to let me open chests in mission 3 and 8, just wouldn't work; please rebellion fix it that's the only negative thing i've got to say about this gorgeous game. multiplayer for the campaign don't really work out well since it's either me or my friend that keeps crashing, two other friends tried it out and same result so please fix this as well. didn't tried out multiplayer and invasion mode yet. lot of new content and ways of playing that I really enjoyed as well. to me it's a 4,65/5

the game is pretty good but it is short. this game had a lot of potential and when you really get into the game it feels like it ends just like that. the graphics are great, missions are fun but just feels rushed. overall great game just to short and leaves you feeling like there could have been more in the story.

It's a sniper elite, if you like playing a sniper in the second world war, buy it on sale.
7/10

An unpolished experience for the stealth game sl*ts amongst us (myself included). Sniper Elite as a franchise never has come close to reaching the heights of Hitman or Metal Gear as far as quality is concerned, but what they do offer is a quick, fun, package of sandbox levels full of unsuspecting Nazis ready to be shot and stabbed away at in solo and coop play. However, with the addition of the best multiplayer invasion mode I've ever seen this side of Dark Souls, the fifth entry into this B tier series feels the most content rich, and easiest to pick up and play.

Great addition to the franchise.  I like the granularity in the difficulty settings, makes it easier to taylor to help mitigate my particular weaknesses.
Tend to always turn off the headline x-ray feature in the series, as I find it incredibly gross, anyway the normal slow death animation is more than enough to satisfy seeing fascist brain splatter.
Still working my way through the campaign, but am expecting it to be as interesting and challenging as previous entries.
An easy recommend for lovers of stealth and long distance violence ;)

i had a great time playing this one and heavily recommend it to everyone, played IGI a lot as a kid and since IGI no other game felt quite the same until sniper elite 5 so in that sense this game felt kinda nostalic, althoug i havent played previous installations of sniper elite. The best thing about this game is that it runs smoothly cause so many mordern games of today demands a super computer from NASA to run, this game is simple and knows its audience, it knows in which direction it wants to go and never tries to be 2 things at once. The story is basic, the music is good, the visuals are good, the game play is simple and smooth, wished there were a few more missions but okay, and i genuienly had fun playing this one, this game is pure vibes. The only negatives i have is that the dlcs are kinda expensive for what they have to offer i mean they could've just included it in the game but anyways i got this game on a sale so no problem. After completion this game gives you a feeling of peace and accomplishment and not unsatisfied and wanting for more...i don't if its a positive or negative, i hope for sniper elite 6 they go for japan in WW2 theme. All in all i would give it a solid 7/10.

I thought this would be your standard run of mill shooter, but I was wrong. It is probably one of the best WWII shooters I have ever played. The importance of strategy and stealth are a big hit for me. Planning where to put traps to cover your exit, finding a soundmask to cover the sound of your weapon and using the right type of ammunition for the job. Plus the long shot x-ray view, whilst gruesome, is a satisfying method of feedback on a job well done.

Very fun game in my opinion. I like having the sniping settings to be realistic while still giving me the cursor so I can aim like a real sniper without being bad at it. The X-ray kills are so satisfying too, I can finally understand the joy Mortal Kombat players have without being bad at the game.

The leap in gameplay mechanism is significant over SE4. Secondary weapons and Side arms can be used in ADS; Non-Lethal methods debuts (no applicable to player-controlled character and Adolf Hitler); special ammunition; in game work bench.
Axis Invasion is a creative attempt in keeping this game alive after 4 years since launch.
Multiplayer community is as toxic as others.

I really enjoy this game, it is very well built. The campaign gives a very generous amount of game time, particularly if you are wanting to 100% it with loads of collectibles and multiple objectives (both mandatory and optional). The game also includes a survival mode and multiplayer if campaigns aren't your thing. I also enjoyed the 5 DLC missions too, but these are obviously a separate purchase.

I fully recommend Sniper Elite 5, if for nothing else than the well renowned x-ray kill camera!

the game is really satisfying and overall nice, i havent encountered a single bug or anything but isnt it a bit unrealistic that a dude goes all over europe against 100-200 people all on his own every time and always manages to outsmart and kill everyone??

[b]First of all. If you're ever stuck between choosing this or Resistance for about the same cost. I personally would say get Resistance.[/b] It just runs better feels smoother and has that bit more polish. Now if this game is cheaper by a good margine then pick this up! It's one of the best versions of this specifically Rebellion style sealth sniper games.

The weapon customization is sufficent though not expansive in my opinion. The gameplay feels solid aside from little detection issues which happen with the npc's where admittiedly they feel a bit too good at finding you. This game will take a mix of movement and action with timing and sealth. This is not about super slow methodical execution but a mixed approach of swiftness of action in sneaky ways.

Shooting feels fun though if you want "[i]hyper realism[/i] sniping" this isn't it. 


The story is the standard fan fare of "Nazi's doing bad things and plans, kill them" so it's par for the course. If that's what you want it's what you will get here. Be aware this is not a long game if you're good at this kind of thing but I'd say more or less around 20-30hrs is the expected time to do the campagin completely makes sense. Not JRPG levels of time but fair enough.

All in all it's a solid pick up on sale. Not sure I would pay full price for this today but... eh?

Very good game. I love sniping Germans in the testicles.
10/10 would recommend.

As a sniper enthusiast who started with the AWP in CS2, the Operator in Valorant, and the DLQ33 in CoD, I realized those games only show part of what real sniping feels like. Real sniping is about patience, wind speed, recoil control, distance judgment, and knowing when to take the shot — or hold it.

This game captures all of that with incredible detail, especially considering it’s set during the World War era. The atmosphere, sound design, and mechanics make the experience feel immersive and authentic. The weapons may be old-school, but every successful shot feels unbelievably satisfying. That dopamine rush from the kill feed never gets old.

I keep coming back to this game again and again. Absolutely worth trying — and the lore won’t disappoint either. 🔫

What a huge disappointment.
The game is ridiculously clunky. I honestly don’t understand how a company that made something as impressive as Sniper Elite 4 could release a game like this.
There’s no way to configure the stick sensitivity in a way that makes the gameplay feel proper or responsive. The character movement feels awkward and heavy. The maps only pretend to be open. There’s no clear logic or consistency to how the game expects you to approach situations.

I’m convinced the goal was to push people into buying expansions in the hope that the game might eventually become enjoyable. I regret the nine hours I wasted trying to turn this game into something worthwhile.

This is honestly deceptive. It’s not a sniper game. Period. It’s a very poor version of Hitman.

doesn't really matter which sniper elite game you buy they are all very consistent and good in their own way - this ones got lots of extra bells and whistles ... very much enjoyed it - you have to be savvy to get all the DLC's cheaply wait for the right sales to come up. you can pretty much buy any of rebbellions games eg: zombie army - strange brigade and so on - all the same engine just cosmetically different enough to be interesting from game to game. consistent is the key word.

Games like these are why I hate Steam's review system. I can only say "did or did not recommend" and not "6 out of 10". SE5 is inferior to 4 in many ways.
Mainly level design. There's too much close-quarters combat and empty space. This is supposed to be a SNIPER game, not a generic 3rd person shooter. And to be fair, there are plenty of sniper spots. But there are too many times where it feels like the game wants you to treat it like a 3PS and not as a SNIPER game.
Nonlethal gameplay options, while they sound nice, are practically worthless. (Metal Gear, Hitman, and Splinter Cell incentivize nonlethal play. This game doesn't.)
What I really hate is the gun customization. It sucks. I hate hate HATE the "this-for-that" style of customization. It's not "tactical", it's annoying. Why can't I just use a suppressor without it messing up every aspect of the gun? 4 let you use Suppressed Ammo and IIRC that didn't screw up bullet velocity or damage, let alone reload time, equip time, time to aim, aim stability... Ugh! 
And while it's nice that we can finally add suppressors to SMGs, it's still ruined by the fact that situations where you want to use a silenced SMG are few and far between. And enemies can STILL hear you from a good distance away. Which is why I put a silencer on the damn thing in the first place!

It's not a bad game, not at all, but it's just not as good or fun as 4.

Bought this game on 19th of OCT 2025 for around 109 rupees , fortunately bought it during  a sale. Its been 7 months today and tbh i really dont get much time to play games , but yes enjoyed this game for 7 months , really liked it , very well optimized it is , atleast on my mid range rig. also the gameplay, BG music , overall graphics and overall the game is 4.5/5. go for this game if u want to play something good. enjoy))))) .
It's a great game, feels tight and visceral. the open world sniping is very fun.
Awesome game for solo amd online players, never gets old, with a fun and supportive online community

fun game with a nice map variety, the dlc maps are smaller and sometimes have some short-wall annoyances but all provide solid long shots. 
Try invasion mode!
well deserved upgrade to its Sniper Elite 4 overall gameplay. I am fan of this series since its original game was launched.
this is just a great game. The stealth is just awesome in terms of quality & experience. My friend & I love this game so much, It was great to get for $7

Hit 100% completion after 150 hours, and SE5 really delivers on the scale. The maps are huge—maybe a bit more linear than before, but the level design is way more fun to play through than previous titles.

I’ve done over 100 invasions too. It’s a blast, though honestly, the invasion loop feels a bit tighter and more fun in Resistance. Still, for the pure single-player experience, these maps are top-tier. If you’re into the long-distance grind, this is it. Satisfying 100% run for sure.

I should start by saying that I’ve never played the previous entries in the series, so my judgment is based solely on this game.

Let’s begin with the graphics: they may not be outstanding, but they’re definitely solid and pleasant overall. Most missions take place outdoors, and the environments are quite varied.

Enemy behavior, as in many games of this type, isn’t exactly top‑tier, though I’ve certainly seen worse. Firing a weapon always produces noise—even with a suppressor—so each gun has its own detection radius within which enemies can hear the shot and become alerted. When an enemy is alarmed, he won’t always take cover right away. If you don’t take him out quickly, he’ll alert everyone nearby, and surrounding enemies will start searching for your position and attempt to take you down.

When multiple enemies are around, you need to decide who to eliminate first or, if that’s not possible, get close and use the quietest weapon you have—or resort to melee. Once an enemy triggers the alarm, a large number of them will begin hunting for you. At that point, relocating quickly becomes essential, because being outnumbered makes it very easy to get hit.

The duration of the alarm varies from mission to mission: sometimes it ends quickly, while in others enemies simply won’t stop searching. In those situations, it’s better not to shoot unless you’re sure you can secure a clean kill and avoid leaving bodies behind. Overall, the challenge level is medium to low; the difficulty comes more from the length of the missions than from their complexity.

As for the weapons, I often stuck with the starting ones, since they’re already quite good. I generally prefer stealth over raw power, trying to stay silent for as long as possible. Weapons can be upgraded with new components, and the further you progress, the better they become. Completing specific actions or finding workbenches grants additional improvements.

The missions are fairly varied, usually consisting of main objectives and a few optional ones. New objectives are often discovered simply by exploring the map, so it’s worth searching every corner instead of focusing only on the markers you already know. Of course, everyone is free to approach the game in their own way.

It took me around 30 hours to finish the main campaign, though there are extra missions available through DLC (which I didn’t purchase).

Would I recommend this game? I had fun with it, and I’m glad I played it through. For me, it easily deserves an 8/10.

Love the sniper elite 5 game. The axis invasion is addicting and killing invaders is super fun as well. But multiplayer sucks. Its called Sniper Elite 5. Not SMG elite or Grenade Throwing Simulator Elite 5.
Game is good, but Welrod is too loud and unrealistic. I do not recommend this weapon, but the game is cool.
Good map layouts, playable both on your own or with others. Challenging but not absurdly so. lots of replayability

This game corrupted my storage and I lost 2TB. If I uninstall it I lose the storage, have to turn the PC off. Unplug the storage then plug it back in. Then I get the storage back but also this stupid game too. It's as if this game is the storage and deleting it means I delete my 2TB. 
Update- Wiped my computer of all data and you would not believe but with literally everything deleted nothing spared Sniper Elite 5 stayed. it took 12 Hours for everything to delete and everything did except this stupid effing game. I have never seen a game act like a virus but this thing is. Yet the worst part is I cannot even play it yet it's still installed. A day will come where I will be gone, you will be gone but Sniper Elite 5 is eternally on this F**king computer.

Good game, but the constant, uninspired rehash of story/setting (WW2 European Theater, Secret Nazi Weapon programs) is extremely annoying. I will not buy any more Sniper Elite games with the same setting/story, even if the gameplay is good - enough is enough.

If you like hanging from a window for 10 minutes because you screwed up hard and brought the entire level to your position only to have an invader spawn in and give you a choice between death and death. Then this IS THE game for you!
An excellent successor to Sniper Elite 4, and the added "Invasion Mode" is a nice touch
delivers a satisfying, refined tactical shooter experience
Wis pokok e sangar tiap momen yang diberikan, suka dengan cerita dan endingnya, gameplay juga makin lama makin seru
Superb graphics and runs well

I am not one to pay alot for a game, but these developers have done an outstand job.  I now have Sniper: Elite V@, Elite 3, Elite 4, Elite 5 and Resistance.  I really enjoy the Elite 5 and Resistance the best.  Will be very happy when they come out with a new version.  Very impressive.  Thank you.
An immersive experience that will make your fingertips sweat and quite simply one of the best games of all time in my opinion.
10/10 game i love the axis fact that people just come into your game to ruin it, brilliant
great game fun and axis invasion mode is the best
Very fun gameplay and unique sniper Jager mode
This is the best game I've ever played. This game is also well-optimized for my PC

The best things of SE5 are the weapon customization, graphics, unit types/ overall look, the skill tree, axis invasion, AI intelligence, etc.
I think the biggest downside of SE5 is half the maps are less sniper missions and more commando missions, which I am ok with, but when you compare these maps to SE4 which had very wide, very maneuverable maps, it's clear as night and day the difference.

There's still plenty of room for long range sniping in SE5, but a lot more of the encounters feel forced as to how you approach and options where you setup are rather limited at a lot of times.
It makes it pretty predictable, especially in axis invasion, to assume where a player sniper will be sitting.
And overall, the tighter maps make CQB more common.

Still, this is my personal favorite SE game.
Military cool-guy man games are not usually my style, but I liked this.
THE REALISM AND FUNNY PHISYCS MAKE THE GAME FUN, SERIOUS, AND CHAOTIC.
it's nice but somehow the previous titles felt better and harder ( 7/10)
Amazing game. Best played at Authentic level
Great casual stealth game if you play it as it is
